skill.name: backend-queries
skill.description: Write secure, optimized database queries using parameterized statements, eager loading, proper indexing, and transaction management. Use this skill when writing database queries, implementing data fetching logic, optimizing query performance, preventing SQL injection, handling N+1 query problems, using ORMs or query builders, implementing caching strategies, or managing database transactions. Apply when working with SQL queries, ORM query methods, database repository patterns, or any code that retrieves or manipulates data from databases.
skill.summary: Write secure, optimized database queries using parameterized statements, eager loading, proper index...
skill.author: EIS-ITS

security_audit.summary: Static path traversal findings are true positives because SKILL.md references a file outside the packaged skill directory. The weak cryptography findings are false positives from prose and do not show cryptographic code or unsafe algorithm use. No prompt injection or confirmed malicious intent was found in the reviewed file.

medium_findings.title: Out-of-Package Relative File Reference
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md points to ../../../agent-os/standards/backend/queries.md. This crosses out of the skill directory, so an assistant could depend on unbundled repository content or read files outside the marketplace package. The target appears to be a standards document, so this is elevated risk rather than confirmed malicious behavior.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The parent-directory traversal is explicit and located in the skill instructions. The referenced path appears documentation-oriented, so confidence is high for the filesystem risk but not for malicious intent.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Matches
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography at SKILL.md lines 3 and 24, but these lines contain natural-language skill description text. No evidence found of cryptographic code, weak algorithm selection, hashing, encryption, or key handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are prose, not executable cryptographic logic. Confidence is very low that this represents an actual cryptography weakness.

dangerous_patterns.title: Parent Directory Traversal in Skill Reference
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses ../../../ in a Markdown link to reference content outside the skill package. This is a filesystem boundary concern for marketplace packaging and review.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The traversal sequence is visible in the reviewed file and directly explains the filesystem risk factor. The context suggests a documentation reference, not a confirmed exploit.

content.value_statement: Database code often collects injection risks, slow queries, and transaction gaps. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward safer and faster data access.
actual_capabilities: Guides use of parameterized queries to reduce SQL injection risk.
Promotes eager loading to avoid N+1 query problems.
Suggests indexes and selected columns for better query performance.
Supports filtering, sorting, pagination, joins, and related data access.
Recommends transaction handling for related database operations.
Covers caching, query timeouts, and performance monitoring practices.
limitations: It provides guidance only; it does not execute or benchmark queries.
It depends on project database schema and ORM conventions supplied by the user.
It does not include the referenced backend standards file in this package.
It cannot guarantee production safety without tests and database review.

use_cases.title: Secure a Repository Query
use_cases.description: Convert unsafe data access into parameterized queries with clear filtering and pagination.

use_cases.title: Improve Slow Data Fetching
use_cases.description: Review query shape, selected columns, eager loading, and indexes for a slow endpoint.

use_cases.title: Plan Reliable Writes
use_cases.description: Design transaction boundaries for related inserts, updates, or deletes.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Simple Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this backend query for SQL injection risk, missing parameters, and unnecessary selected data. Explain the safest rewrite.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Pagination and Sorting
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me implement filtering, sorting, and pagination for this list endpoint. Keep the query parameterized and predictable.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Related Data Loading
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this data fetching flow for N+1 queries, missing eager loading, and useful indexes. Recommend changes in priority order.

prompt_templates.title: Design Transaction Boundaries
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this multi-step database write. Define the transaction boundary, failure behavior, locks, timeouts, and consistency risks.

output_examples.input: A controller builds a user search query from request fields.
output_examples.output: The response identifies injection risk, recommends named parameters, limits selected columns, and adds stable pagination.

output_examples.input: An endpoint loads orders and then loads customer data inside a loop.
output_examples.output: The response flags an N+1 pattern, proposes eager loading, and suggests indexes for the join and sort columns.

output_examples.input: A service creates an invoice, line items, and inventory updates.
output_examples.output: The response defines one transaction, rollback behavior, timeout expectations, and checks for concurrent inventory changes.

best_practices: Provide schema details, indexes, and ORM names before asking for query guidance.
Ask for security, performance, and transaction review together for database changes.
Validate generated query changes with tests, query plans, and realistic data volumes.
anti_patterns: Do not concatenate request values into raw SQL strings.
Do not fetch every column when an endpoint needs only a few fields.
Do not split related writes across separate commits without a clear consistency reason.

faq.question: Does this skill write database code?
faq.answer: It guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code while they write or review database access code.

faq.question: Which databases does it support?
faq.answer: The guidance is general and applies to SQL databases, ORMs, query builders, and repository patterns.

faq.question: Can it prevent every SQL injection issue?
faq.answer: No. It promotes safer patterns, but teams still need code review, tests, and database-specific validation.

faq.question: Does it optimize queries automatically?
faq.answer: No. It suggests query improvements, but actual performance depends on schema, indexes, data size, and execution plans.

faq.question: Why is there a security warning?
faq.answer: The skill references a file outside its package using a parent-directory path. That needs publisher review.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It has no confirmed malicious behavior, but the external relative file reference should be documented or bundled.
